items.text:
Subject: Overdue notice — "Patterns of Urban Migration"

Dear Jamal,

Our records show that the title "Patterns of Urban Migration" was due back at Brookfield Library on Tuesday, May 18, and remains checked out under your account. Please return or renew it by Friday, May 28 to avoid additional charges.

As a reminder of the current policy:

- Items overdue by one to seven days carry a $0.50 daily fee.
- Items overdue by more than two weeks add a $5.00 restock charge.
- Holds on your account will prevent transcript requests until the balance is cleared.

If you no longer have the book, please stop by the circulation desk at Sterling 130 between 9:00 AM and 4:30 PM, Monday through Friday, and ask about our lost-item process. Renewals may also be completed online through the campus library portal at lib.campus.edu using your university ID.

Thank you for your prompt attention to this matter.

Best regards,
Ms. Ortega
Circulation Supervisor

format_metadata.subject: Overdue notice — Patterns of Urban Migration

questions.stem: According to the email, what fee applies during the first week an item is overdue?

options.A: A flat $5.00 restock charge at pickup
options.B: A weekly $2.50 charge for renewal requests
options.C: A daily $0.50 charge for each late day
options.D: A one-time $7.00 fee at the circulation desk

questions.explanation: The email lists items overdue by one to seven days as carrying a $0.50 daily fee.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about Jamal if he ignores the notice?

options.A: His library card will be suspended automatically
options.B: He may face holds that block transcript requests
options.C: He will be charged for items returned in good condition
options.D: He will lose access to the online library portal

questions.explanation: The email states that holds on the account will prevent transcript requests until the balance is cleared, so ignoring the notice may produce such holds.

questions.stem: What is the main purpose of the email?

options.A: To remind Jamal to return or renew an overdue title
options.B: To announce a campus-wide change in library policy
options.C: To invite Jamal to a circulation desk policy meeting
options.D: To confirm that Jamal's lost-item request was approved

questions.explanation: The email opens by noting the book is overdue and asks Jamal to return or renew it, with the rest of the message supporting that request.

items.text:
Subject: Possible shift swap for Thursday, June 11

Hi Priscilla,

I hope your week is going well. I'm scheduled for the evening shift at the campus information desk on Thursday, June 11, from 4:00 PM to 9:00 PM, but my study group for the EDU 240 midterm has just been moved to that same window, and I cannot reschedule with my group.

I noticed that you're listed for the Sunday afternoon shift on June 14, from noon to 5:00 PM. Would you be willing to swap shifts with me? I would happily take your Sunday slot in exchange for covering my Thursday evening.

A few notes that might help you decide:

- The Thursday evening desk is usually quiet after 6:00 PM, with mostly directional questions.
- The Sunday afternoon shift includes one mandatory walkthrough with the security supervisor at 1:30 PM.
- Either of us would need to file the change with Mr. Larson through the staff portal at least 24 hours in advance.

Let me know by Tuesday evening if this could work for you, and I'll handle the paperwork. Thanks so much for considering it!

Thanks,
Darnell

format_metadata.subject: Possible shift swap for Thursday, June 11

questions.stem: According to the email, what extra task is attached to Priscilla's Sunday shift?

options.A: A safety quiz given by the staff coordinator
options.B: A mandatory inventory check before opening
options.C: A short orientation for visiting parents on tours
options.D: A walkthrough with the security supervisor at 1:30 PM

questions.explanation: The email notes that the Sunday afternoon shift includes one mandatory walkthrough with the security supervisor at 1:30 PM.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about how the swap will be processed?

options.A: The supervisor will make the change at the desk on Thursday
options.B: The swap must be submitted through the staff portal in advance
options.C: The decision rests entirely with the security supervisor's office
options.D: The change will take effect automatically when the shift begins

questions.explanation: The email states that either of them would need to file the change with Mr. Larson through the staff portal at least 24 hours in advance.

questions.stem: What is the main purpose of the email?

options.A: To complain about an unfair scheduling decision
options.B: To announce a change in evening desk hours
options.C: To propose trading two specific work shifts
options.D: To request approval for a personal day off

questions.explanation: The email's central request is the swap of Darnell's Thursday shift with Priscilla's Sunday shift, with details supporting that request.
